member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.FirstChanceExceptionPool
member.summary: Collects managed exceptions via FirstChanceException and keeps them in a per-thread FIFO queue for later correlation with CLR catch events.
member.remarks:
Thread-safety: OnFirstChanceException runs on the throwing thread; DrainPending() runs on the listener's thread and guarded by lock.
Capture scope: consumers wrap their capture call in EnterCaptureScope(). Any FirstChanceException that fires on the same thread inside that scope is dropped, preventing feedback loops if the capture path itself throws.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.FirstChanceExceptionPool.RemoveThreads(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Int64})
member.remarks: Called by CoreClrExceptionHandler to clean up dead threads from the pending list.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.CoreClrExceptionHandler
member.summary: Detects "unhandled" C# exceptions in Godot by combining FirstChanceException with .NET runtime EventSource events. When ExceptionCatchStart reports a Godot bridge method as the catcher, the exception is classified as unhandled by user code and sent to Sentry.
member.remarks:
Key insight: FirstChanceException runs on the throwing thread, but EventListener callbacks run on a dedicated background thread. We correlate via OS thread ID. A FIFO queue per thread is needed because multiple FirstChanceException events fire synchronously before the EventListener processes any ExceptionCatchStart(250).
ExceptionThrown(80) is emitted before FirstChanceException, but their arrival order on the listener side is racey, so we must not depend on it. The only reliable ordering here is that both arrive before ExceptionCatchStart(250).
See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/fundamentals/diagnostics/runtime-exception-events

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.CoreClrExceptionHandler.HandleExceptionThrown(System.Diagnostics.Tracing.EventWrittenEventArgs)
member.remarks: Runs on the listener thread.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.CoreClrExceptionHandler.HandleExceptionCatchStart(System.Diagnostics.Tracing.EventWrittenEventArgs)
member.remarks: Runs on the listener thread.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.CoreClrExceptionHandler.CleanupDeadThreads
member.remarks: Dead threads observed this cycle are removed next cycle, so in-flight events have time to drain.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.CoreClrExceptionHandler.GetGodotBridgeName(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the display name of the Godot bridge class if the method signature matches a known bridge catch site, or null if it's not a bridge method.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.LoggerExceptionHandler
member.summary: Uses FirstChanceException to capture the last exception per thread, and native Godot Logger to detect when Godot's bridge catches one. When the native logger detects C# error, we capture the cached exception.
member.remarks: Note that, Godot doesn't log .NET exceptions when its debugger is active, so this handler alone is not sufficient on desktop platforms during debugging. That's why CoreClrExceptionHandler also exists - it captures exceptions even with the debugger attached, but only on CoreCLR (PC/Mac).

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.ExceptionHandling.NativeThreadId
member.summary: Cross-platform OS thread ID retrieval.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Internal.CocoaDebugImagesProcessor
member.summary: Attaches Mach-O debug images to events on iOS so NativeAOT stack frames symbolicate server-side.
member.remarks: Frames arrive with image_addr set but no matching debug_meta.images entries, leaving the symbolicator with nothing to bind to. This processor reads the images from the in-process sentry-cocoa cache and appends them to the event.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Internal.DefaultAttachmentsProcessor
member.summary: Adds default attachments managed and created by native integrations (log, screenshot and view hierarchy) to .NET events.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Internal.GodotAssemblyReader
member.summary: Provides assembly bytes to the Sentry .NET SDK for managed stack-trace symbolication.
member.remarks:
On Android, .NET assemblies live inside the application archive (possibly nested in a .pck) and have no on-disk path the SDK can open. Without their bytes the SDK cannot build debug images, so the server reports every managed frame as unknown_image. This reader is designed to fetch bytes via the native bridge, routed through Godot's FileAccess so the engine's virtual FS handles packing and decompression.
The SDK only reads two small segments per assembly (PE headers at the start and the debug directory near the end), so each assembly is exposed as a seekable Stream rather than whole. Touched segments are cached per assembly, and repeat captures are served from cache and do no further I/O.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Internal.GodotAssemblyReader.TryReadAssembly(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a PEReader over the named assembly, or null if it cannot be resolved.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Internal.GodotAssemblyReader.AssemblyReadStream
member.summary: Seekable stream over a managed assembly in Godot's virtual FS.
member.remarks: Reads hit cached segments when possible, and misses go to the native file handle and the segment is stored so later captures hit our cache. The handle is opened lazily and closed on dispose.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Internal.GodotAssemblyReader.AssemblyReadStream.#ctor(System.String,Sentry.Godot.Internal.GodotAssemblyReader.AssemblyReadState,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Seekable stream over a managed assembly in Godot's virtual FS.
member.remarks: Reads hit cached segments when possible, and misses go to the native file handle and the segment is stored so later captures hit our cache. The handle is opened lazily and closed on dispose.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Internal.GodotLog
member.summary: Wrapper around native logging functions via P/Invoke.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Internal.SentryGodotInitializer
member.summary: Handles automatic initialization of the .NET Sentry SDK. Called from [ModuleInitializer] in SentryAutoInit.cs, which is compiled into the user's assembly.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Interop.GodotScopeObserver
member.summary: Synchronizes scope changes to native layer.
member.remarks: Local scope changes are NOT synced, which prevents them from leaking into the native current scope.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge
member.summary: Handles native layer SDK operations via P/Invoke.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.MarshallStringMap(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.String},Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.ManagedStringMapAction)
member.summary: Marshals a dictionary into a stack-allocated interleaved buffer and passes the resulting struct to a native function call. The map is only valid for the duration of the call (owned by managed code).

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.IsNativeAvailable
member.summary: Checks whether the native interop library is loaded and exposes the expected symbol. Returns false when running outside the Godot/GDExtension host, so initialization can be skipped.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.RegisterManagedFunctions
member.summary: Registers managed functions that are called from native layer.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.ProcessNativeEventCallback(System.IntPtr)
member.remarks: Called by native for each native/engine event. Runs the options.Native.SetBeforeSend callback with a temporary event wrapper. Returns 1 to keep the event, 0 to discard.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.FetchDefaultAttachments(Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions)
member.summary: Adds default attachments resolved by the native layer after init() to the managed options.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.IsAndroid
member.remarks: OperatingSystem.IsAndroid() returns false on Godot Android.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.OpenManagedAssembly(System.String)
member.summary: Opens managed assembly by name from Godot's virtual filesystem as a seekable handle. Returns null handle with zero length if the assembly was not found.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.ReadManagedAssembly(System.IntPtr,System.Int64,System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Reads bytes from an open assembly handle at specified offset into destination. Returns the number of bytes read, which may be less than requested at EOF.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.CloseManagedAssembly(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Closes a managed assembly handle opened by .

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.GetCocoaDebugImages(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{System.Int64})
member.summary: Resolves Mach-O debug images for the given image base addresses from the Cocoa SDK's in-process cache. Used to populate debug_meta.images for NativeAOT frames on iOS, which sentry-dotnet would otherwise leave empty for our TFM-neutral build.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeBridge.csharp_interop_get_sdk_version
member.remarks: Returns compile-time literal - don't free!

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeLibResolver
member.summary: Resolves the sentry-godot GDExtension library at runtime.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.Interop.NativeLibResolver.Resolve
member.summary: Returns the GDExtension library handle for P/Invokes and the availability probe.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.Native
member.summary: Configuration that applies to the native layer, including the before-send hook for native events such as engine, GDExtension and GDScript errors.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.AttachLog
member.summary: If enabled, the SDK will attach the Godot log file to the event.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.AttachSceneTree
member.summary: If enabled, the SDK captures scene tree hierarchy data with each event.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.AttachScreenshot
member.summary: If enabled, the SDK captures screenshots for events meeting or exceeding .
member.remarks: This feature is experimental and may impact performance. Test before enabling in production.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.ScreenshotLevel
member.summary: Minimum event level for which screenshots are captured.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.EnableAppHangTracking
member.summary: If enabled, the SDK monitors the main thread and reports hang events when it becomes unresponsive for longer than .
member.remarks: This feature applies to iOS and macOS only. On Android, configures ANR (Application Not Responding) detection instead.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.AppHangTimeout
member.summary: Duration after which the application is considered to have hanged.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.GodotLogger
member.summary: Configures the capture of Godot errors and log messages as Sentry events, breadcrumbs, and logs. See .

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.Android
member.summary: Configures Android-specific options, such as ANR (Application Not Responding) detection.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.ApplyNativeOptions
member.summary: Reads resolved options from the native SentryOptions.
member.remarks: Since the native SDK initializes before .NET, treat native options as single source of truth for those options that are common to both.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.ApplyNativeOptionsDefaults
member.summary: Applies default option values from the native layer, which may be overridden by the user callback.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions.ApplyTemplateSubstitutions
member.summary: Applies template substitutions to options that may have been set by user callback.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.GodotLoggerEventMask
member.summary: Bitfield mask for Godot logger events.
member.remarks: Used to specify which Godot logger events are captured as Sentry events, breadcrumbs and logs.

member.name: F:Sentry.Godot.GodotLoggerEventMask.None
member.summary: No logger errors or messages will be captured.

member.name: F:Sentry.Godot.GodotLoggerEventMask.Error
member.summary: Native (C++) and engine errors, which may also originate from a script.

member.name: F:Sentry.Godot.GodotLoggerEventMask.Warning
member.summary: Warnings.

member.name: F:Sentry.Godot.GodotLoggerEventMask.Script
member.summary: Script errors.

member.name: F:Sentry.Godot.GodotLoggerEventMask.Shader
member.summary: Shader errors.

member.name: F:Sentry.Godot.GodotLoggerEventMask.Message
member.summary: Log messages such as GD.Print() statements.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions
member.summary: Contains configuration options that control how the SDK captures Godot errors and log messages as Sentry events, breadcrumbs, and logs. Access this configuration through .
member.remarks: This includes engine errors, GDScript and shader errors, GDExtension errors, GD.PushError() and GD.PushWarning() statements, as well as log messages from GD.Print() and similar functions. These options control Godot-side error reporting, not .NET errors.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions.Enabled
member.summary: If enabled, the SDK will capture logged errors as events, breadcrumbs and/or logs, as defined by , and . Crashes are always captured.
member.remarks: This setting controls GDScript/engine error reporting, not .NET errors.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions.IncludeSourceContext
member.summary: If enabled, the SDK will include the surrounding source code of logged errors, if available in the exported project.
member.remarks: This setting controls GDScript error reporting, not .NET errors.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions.IncludeVariables
member.summary: If enabled, the SDK will include local variables from stack traces when capturing script errors. This allows showing the values of variables at each frame in the call stack. Requires enabling the debug/settings/gdscript/always_track_local_variables project setting.
member.remarks: May impact performance, especially for applications with frequent errors or deep call stacks. This setting controls GDScript error reporting, not .NET errors.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions.EventMask
member.summary: Specifies the Godot logger events that are automatically captured as Sentry events. Accepts a single value or a bitwise combination of masks.
member.remarks: has no effect here. To capture log messages, set it in and/or instead.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions.BreadcrumbMask
member.summary: Specifies the Godot logger events that are automatically captured as Sentry breadcrumbs. Accepts a single value or a bitwise combination of masks.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions.LogMask
member.summary: Specifies the Godot logger events that are automatically captured as Sentry logs. Accepts a single value or a bitwise combination of masks. Empty by default, so no events are captured as logs.
member.remarks: Log capture requires to be enabled.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotLoggerOptions.Limits
member.summary: Defines throttling limits for the error logger. These limits are used to prevent the SDK from sending too many non-critical and repeating error events. See .
member.remarks: This setting controls GDScript/engine error reporting, not .NET errors.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.SentryLoggerLimits
member.summary: Specifies throttling limits for the error logger. These limits govern the behavior of throttling and are used to prevent the SDK from sending too many non-critical and repeating error events.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryLoggerLimits.EventsPerFrame
member.summary: Specifies the maximum number of error events to send per processed frame. If exceeded, no further errors will be captured until the next frame.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryLoggerLimits.RepeatedErrorWindow
member.summary: Specifies the minimum time interval between two identical errors. If exceeded, no further errors from the same line of code with the identical message will be captured until the next interval. Set to zero to disable this limit.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryLoggerLimits.ThrottleEvents
member.summary: Specifies the maximum number of events allowed within a sliding time window of . If exceeded, errors will be captured as breadcrumbs only until capacity is freed.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryLoggerLimits.ThrottleWindow
member.summary: Specifies the time window for . Set to zero to disable this limit.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.SentryAndroidOptions
member.summary: Contains configuration options that apply only when the project runs on Android, such as ANR (Application Not Responding) detection. Access this configuration through .

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryAndroidOptions.EnableAnrDetection
member.summary: Enables detection and reporting of ANR (Application Not Responding) errors. The SDK monitors the main thread for unresponsiveness and reports an event when an ANR occurs.
member.remarks: Android 11 and later use the system-based V2 implementation, while earlier versions use the watchdog-based V1 implementation. See and for options specific to each implementation. On Apple platforms, configures the equivalent app hang detection. To learn more, visit Application Not Responding documentation.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryAndroidOptions.AnrTimeoutInterval
member.summary: Specifies how long the main thread must stay blocked before the SDK reports an ANR.
member.remarks: Applies only when is enabled, and only to the V1 implementation used on Android versions before 11. On Android 11 and later, the operating system determines when the application stops responding, so this value has no effect.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryAndroidOptions.AttachAnrThreadDump
member.summary: Attaches the operating system's thread dump to the ANR event as a plain-text attachment, adding detail for investigating where the application became unresponsive.
member.remarks: Applies only when is enabled, and only to the V2 implementation used on Android 11 and later.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent
member.summary: Represents a native Godot event passed to the before-send callback registered through .
member.remarks: This instance is valid only while the callback is running and must not be retained. Each member reads from or writes to the live native event across the interop boundary, so only the fields a callback touches cross it.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Id
member.summary: The event's unique identifier.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Platform
member.summary: The platform that produced the event.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Message
member.summary: The message that describes the event.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Level
member.summary: The severity of the event.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Logger
member.summary: The name of the logger that captured the event.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Release
member.summary: The release version of the application.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Distribution
member.summary: The distribution of the application.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.Environment
member.summary: The environment the event was captured in.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.GetTag(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the value of the tag with the given key, or null if the tag is not set.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.SetTag(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the tag with the given key to the given value.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.UnsetTag(System.String)
member.summary: Removes the tag with the given key.

member.name: P:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.ExceptionCount
member.summary: The number of exceptions attached to the event.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.GetExceptionValue(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the message of the exception at the given index, or null if the index is out of range.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent.SetExceptionValue(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the message of the exception at the given index.

member.name: T:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeOptions
member.summary: Contains options for the native layer, accessed through .
member.remarks: The native layer handles events that originate outside .NET, such as GDScript and engine errors.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeOptions.SetBeforeSend(System.Func{Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent,Sentry.Godot.SentryNativeEvent})
member.summary: Sets the callback invoked before a native event is sent, allowing you to inspect, modify, or discard it.
member.remarks: Native events include GDScript and engine errors. The callback receives a ; return it to send the event, or return null to discard it. Native crash events are not passed to this callback. Managed (.NET) events are configured through instead.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentrySdk.Init(System.Action{Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions})
member.summary: Initializes the .NET SDK with an optional configuration callback.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentrySdk.InitFromNative
member.summary: Initializes the .NET SDK using resolved options from the native layer.
member.remarks: Always called by the native SDK after initialization, but should only take effect when the native layer is auto-initialized or initialized from GDScript.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentrySdk.ConfigureAssemblyReader(Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions)
member.summary: On Android, lets the SDK read managed assembly bytes from the packed project data.
member.remarks: Godot's Mono runtime on Android loads managed assemblies from the packed project data, so they have no on-disk path and the SDK cannot build debug images for managed stack frames. The reader resolves the bytes through the native layer instead. Desktop builds keep the SDK's default disk reader, which finds the assemblies as loose files next to the executable.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentrySdk.ConfigureCocoaDebugImagesOnIOS(Sentry.Godot.SentryGodotOptions)
member.summary: On iOS, registers a processor that attaches Mach-O debug images from the in-process Cocoa SDK so NativeAOT stack frames can be symbolicated server-side.

member.name: M:Sentry.Godot.SentrySdk.Close
member.summary: Flushes the events and disables the SDK.
